questions.q: The President of India enjoys the power to grant pardons, reprieves, etc. under which article of the Constitution?
The President of India is directly elected by the people of India through a method of voting. Which of the following statements is correct?
The President of India serves as the head of the state and performs various ceremonial functions such as hosting state and official guests. What is the main purpose of these functions?
questions.answer: The main purpose of these functions is to showcase India's culture and traditions to international guests and enhance its diplomatic relations.

questions.q: What is the process by which the President of India can be removed from office?
questions.answer: The President of India can be removed from office through impeachment proceedings. This process involves a formal charge being brought against the President by both Houses of Parliament, followed by a trial in the Rashtrapati Bhavan. If the President is found guilty, they can be removed from office.

questions.q: The Governor of a state in India is appointed by the President of India. What is the main role of the Governor?
questions.answer: The main role of the Governor is to act as the representative of the President in the state and to ensure that the Constitution is implemented in the state.

questions.q: Who among the following is not a part of the Council of Ministers?
questions.answer: The Speaker of the Lok Sabha

questions.q: The Cabinet Minister is responsible for a particular department or ministry. What is the process by which the Cabinet Minister is appointed?
questions.answer: By the President, after consulting the Prime Minister and other Ministers in the Cabinet

questions.q: The President of India has the power to declare a national emergency under which article of the Constitution?
What is the name of the institution that advises the President of India on important matters of the state?
questions.answer: The Prime Minister and the Cabinet of Ministers, along with the Council of Ministers, advise the President on important matters of the state.

questions.q: The President of India can dissolve the Lok Sabha under which article of the Constitution?
